project_name: Swedish Covenant Hospital ER/Women's Floor Rehab RDA Amendment
project_description:
This RDA Amendment will allow additional time to complete the Emergency Department modernization and allow some of the TIF payments to be made during construction at milestones approved by the IL Dept. of Public Health.  The original RDA called for payments to commence upon the issuance of the Certificate of completion.

Women's Health Care Center:
SCH plans to expand and enhance clinical services by removing access barriers for women of every ethnic and religious group.  The anchor for this initiative is building the Mayora Rosenberg Women's Health Center on the 4th floor of the Galter Medical Pavilion.  The 4th floor will be totally redone to provide more than 13,000 square feet for a Women's Health Center, a Resource Center, and outpatient Cardiology Center for Women, and class and conference rooms  A new women's health center provides the opportunity to centralize in one area all services for women, provide an all� female staff and women only patient areas, scheduling with evening and weekend appointments, child friendly areas, bilingual staff and bi-lingual educational health care resource materials.

Emergency Department:
Emergency Department Renovation will consist of a "gut rehab" of approximately 14,603 square feet on the 1st floor of the East Wing building.  Specific improvements include 15 private large rooms for trauma cases, 5 dedicated psychiatric rooms, 12-bay fast track private exam areas, new post-exam common waiting area, private consultation room, new pharmacy work station, new physician/nurse work station, and improvements to the infrastructure that serves the emergency room (e.g. elevators, mechanical systems, communications, and restrooms).

Located in the Uptown community, the project rehabilitated 77 affordable multi-family housing rental units across three buildings located at 4431 N. Clifton, 900 W. Windsor and 927 W. Wilson Avenue. The first floor includes an office, storage for tenants, two sunrooms and a community room for tenants. 

There are 33 units that are subsidized by the DOH"s Trust Fund and serve households with incomes at or below 30% of AMI. Residents for Effective Shelter Transition (REST) is an organization that provides rental subsidy funds to formally homeless households with Shelter Plus Care funds. REST will continue to subsidize approximately 20 units with these funds at the Wilson and Windsor buildings. 

The Low-Income Project shall mean 77 units in the Project financed with CDBG funds are required to be occupied by Low-Income Families. All units are required to be at or below 60% of AMI.

Earle School, LLC will acquire the former public school building, constructed circa 1897, from Chicago Public Schools and adaptively reuse it to create high-quality supportive housing in the Englewood community. The development will provide 50 units of Permanent Supportive Housing (PSH), all of which will be affordable to households earning no more than 60% of the Area Median Income (AMI). The project is designed to serve individuals and families experiencing or at risk of homelessness, pairing housing with on-site supportive services. Fifteen of the units will be assisted through HUD Section 811, while nine units will be supported through CLIHTF-MAUI, providing deep affordability at the 15% AMI level. Residents will have access to a full suite of amenities, including on-site property management, social services, laundry facilities, a community room, exercise room, computer lab, and other shared spaces. The property is conveniently located near the 63rd Street bus line, offering direct access to neighborhood businesses and services along 63rd Street. The project has been awarded Low-Income Housing Tax Credits (LIHTC) through the IHDA funding round and will also utilize Historic Tax Credits to preserve and restore the historic character of the 1897 school building.

The Developer 4300 Roosevelt LLC and partnership between Related Midwest LLC and 548 Development is proposing to redevelop the property into a two, 181,760 square foot warehouse buildings that are intended for industrial users such as warehouse, shipping and logistics.  No tenant has been identified yet. Located in North Lawndale, the project includes the new construction of one five-story building containing 90 affordable rental units for homeless and disabled Veterans.  The project will provide 88 studios and two one-bedroom units affordable to households earning at or below 60% of the area median income.  There will be 26 on-site parking spaces at the rear of the building.  In addition to many supportive social services being offered at this site, including substance abuse and mental health counseling, income supports, job training, the developer has an office one block east of this development which will house a Community Referral and Resource Center (CRRC).

developer: Bickerdike Redevelopment Corporation or related entity(ies)
project_description: Bickerdike Redevelopment Corporation is proposing the development of a 100% affordable, multifamily transit-served rental apartment building at 5853 N Broadway in the Edgewater community. The site is a city-owned parcel currently serving as the 48th Ward Streets and Sanitation Yard, located at the heart of the Edgewater neighborhood directly adjacent to the Thorndale Red Line Station and within walking distance of Broadway Armory Park, schools, shops, restaurants, and the lakefront. This development plan calls for an 11-story building with 90 affordable apartments serving households at or below 60% of the Area Median Income (AMI), including 5% of units set aside for permanent supportive housing. The unit mix will include 10 three-bedroom, 50 two-bedroom, and 30 one-bedroom apartments. The project responds to rising rents, displacement, and the loss of naturally occurring affordable housing in Edgewater, as well as recommendations in the Broadway Land Use Framework (adopted by the Chicago Plan Commission in 2025), which identifies this segment of Broadway as a priority area for dense, mixed-use, transit-oriented housing. The proposal was selected for a conditional award of City financial assistance in connection with the Department of Housing�s 2023 funding round.
Construction of public infrastructure that will address long standing needs and facilitate a new 13,000,000 square foot mixed use development.

This master site contains two buildings totaling 120 units contemplated over two phases. This PROMA entry is for PHASE I, which will consist of 62 units intended as workforce housing and will include two ground floor 1-bedroom live/work units.

Construction of 43-unit affordable building with 1-, 2- and 3 bedroom with community and retail space on the first floor. Of the 43 units, 15 are 1-bedrooms, 19 are 2-bedrons and 9 are 3-bedrooms. 11 units will be affordable at 30% AM,I 23 at 60% AMI and 9 at 80% AMI.

developer: Hispanic Housing Development Corporation, or related entity(ies)
project_description: Hispanic Housing Development Corporation seeks to redevelop the vacant historic Pioneer Arcade property and land at 1539 N Pulaski Rd into a 6-story, 61-unit affordable rental building with supportive services for very-low-income elderly households, providing 58 one-bedroom units at 50% AMI or less, 2 two-bedroom units at 60% AMI or less, and 1 two-bedroom unit for a resident manager for a total of 61 units. The project will preserve the terracotta facade of the historic Pioneer Arcade property and retain approximately one-third of the existing structure representing the east portion of the ground floor lobby and 2nd floor assembly space, behind which will be the attached new construction rental development and a total of (26) parking spaces on-site, (4) of which are accessible. The proposed City financial assistance is TIF and Adopt-a-Landmark funding; this project was selected for LIHTC by IHDA and for Section 202 Supportive Housing for the Elderly funding via HUD competitive process.

developer: IFF Real Estate Holdings, LLC + Foundation for Homan Square
project_description: Preservation and moderate rehabilitation of six three-story multifamily buildings at 3607, 3621, and 3645 W. Polk St.; 906 and 908 S. Central Park Ave.; and 921 S. Lawndale Ave. within the Homan-Arthington TIF area, preserving 107 affordable rental units. Project is supported by City financial assistance, including $9.99M in TIF, ~$12.1M in City-allocated 4% LIHTC equity, and an ~$20M City tax-exempt bond issuance, and includes interior, exterior, and site upgrades to improve building longevity, energy efficiency, accessibility, and life safety.

This project will redevelop an existing 6,527 SF, common brick building into an arts hub. The project will offer a multi-purpose space to musicians, community-focused arts organizations, emerging young artists, and other creatively inclined residents of Albany Park. Rehabilitation work will include mechanical, electrical, and plumbing replacement; masonry repairs; the installation of energy-efficient storefront systems; roofing repairs and weatherproofing; and interior outfitting.

The project includes the rehabilitation of the Medinah Temple Building, Tree Studios Building, Unit Building, and the interior courtyard.  The project constructed a 120,000 square foot home goods store in the Medinah Temple Building and commercial rental property in Unit Building and Tree Studios Building.

project_name: ASPIRE Center / West Side Health RDA
project_description: Located at the corner of Madison St. and Central Avenue (5500 W. Madison), the Aspire Center for Workforce Innovation is a multiphase development project to renovate and re purpose the former Robert Emmet Public Elementary School into a coordinated community hub with industry sector training center that will service youth and out-of-work individuals with in-demand skill sets and small business supports, as well as restaurant space and a local bank.
